que: In Unit Testing on Android, what is the primary goal?
options: avoid understanding requirements
replace testing entirely
skip state/data modeling
test business logic on JVM quickly

que: Which API/concept is most central to Unit Testing on Android?
options: JUnit
legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic
manual polling loop for all tasks
random utility class name

que: What is a recommended best practice for Unit Testing on Android?
options: mock dependencies and assert outcomes
ignore edge/error states
mix unrelated responsibilities in one function
optimize before verifying correctness

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing Unit Testing on Android?
options: requiring emulator for pure unit tests
keep boundaries clear
follow official docs for API usage
write focused tests for behavior

que: For production code, which approach fits Unit Testing on Android best?
options: Use Unit Testing on Android while bypassing architecture boundaries
Use Unit Testing on Android and suppress all warnings
Use Unit Testing on Android only as theory with no implementation
Use Unit Testing on Android with clear state flow and testable logic

que: A teammate asks where to start with Unit Testing on Android. What is best advice?
options: copy an unknown snippet without context
skip running the app after changes
avoid feedback from tests/review
Read official docs (https://developer.android.com/training/testing/local-tests) and implement a small sample

que: Which statement about Unit Testing on Android is most accurate?
options: Unit Testing on Android should be applied identically in every layer
Unit Testing on Android removes the need for any testing
Unit Testing on Android is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data
Unit Testing on Android is mainly about adding boilerplate

que: In a code review for Unit Testing on Android, what would you flag?
options: traceable data flow between layers
small pure functions with clear names
explicit modeling of UI/data states
Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using Unit Testing on Android correctly?
options: A small, working feature that demonstrates JUnit in context
a slide deck with no runnable code
an implementation with no error states
a large refactor without tests

que: To learn Unit Testing on Android deeply, what should you do next?
options: memorize definitions only
Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using Unit Testing on Android patterns
avoid official references
skip verification to save time
